role: IRGC-Quds Force senior officer; directed 2011 Arbabsiar assassination plot; deployed to Yemen
bio:
Abdul Reza Shahlai is a senior officer in the [IRGC-Quds Force](/orgs/irgc-quds-force) who has been sanctioned by the United States, the European Union, and the United Kingdom for his role in directing terrorist operations abroad. According to US prosecutors, Shahlai directed [Gholam Shakuri](/people/gholam-shakuri) in the [2011 plot to assassinate Saudi Ambassador Adel Al-Jubeir](/events/arbabsiar-plot-2011) in Washington DC, making him the senior IRGC-QF officer behind the operation.

Shahlai is also accused of coordinating a 2007 attack on US military personnel in Karbala, Iraq that killed five American soldiers. The US Department of State offered a $15 million reward through its Rewards for Justice programme for information on Shahlai's operations and networks. He was subsequently deployed to Yemen, where he coordinates IRGC-QF support for Houthi forces.

On the night of the [assassination of Qassem Soleimani](/events/soleimani-assassination-2020) in January 2020, the United States simultaneously attempted a strike targeting Shahlai in Yemen, but the operation failed to kill him.

sources.title: Abdul Reza Shahlai — Counter Extremism Project
Abdul Reza Shahla'i — Rewards for Justice
U.S. Strike on Iranian Commander in Yemen the Night of Suleimani's Assassination Killed the Wrong Man

editorial_notes: Sanctioned by US (Oct 2011, EO 13224), EU (Oct 2011), UK (Oct 2011). $15M Rewards for Justice bounty. Failed US strike in Yemen on night of Soleimani assassination (Jan 2020). Currently believed to be IRGC-QF coordinator in Yemen.
